# Common Origin — Brand Identity

> This document is the primary brand context for all agents operating in this repository.
> Read this file in full before making any decision that touches component design, naming, visual style, or user experience.

---

## What is Common Origin?

Common Origin is the personal creative studio of one person: a music producer, digital designer, graphic designer, product designer, front-end developer, and creative coder — all the same person, with 15+ years of professional creative practice across all of these disciplines.

The name is literal. All creative output — music, visual design, generative art, software, writing — has a *common origin*. One person. Many forms.

**The defining sentence of this brand:**
> "Personal creativity has many forms but one origin."

This is not a commercial brand optimising for growth metrics, followers, or revenue. It is a personal creative studio built for full creative freedom and ownership. This authenticity is not a detail — it is the foundation. Everything the design system produces should reflect this: considered, intentional, not trying to impress, not performing ambition.

---

## Origin Story

The founder has been a music producer for over 15 years — signed releases on notable record labels, international touring, and deep involvement in the electronic music world. After starting a family, the desire to tour and release through external labels diminished. The intent became: full creative control, full ownership of output.

Simultaneously, 15+ years of design practice (digital, graphic, product, creative coding) created a parallel creative body of work.

Common Origin is the brand that holds it all — the intersection of music, visual art, code, and design. The brand was created to release music and creative work without external pressure, timeline, or expectation.

---

## Brand Philosophy

From the original designer brief, these are the concepts at the core of the brand:

- **Entities with multiple parts making a whole** — the brand itself is a union of disciplines
- **Cell reproduction, evolution, layers overlapping** — growth from a single origin point
- **The idea that humans, animals, and life are all connected** — universality through shared origin
- **The intersection is the point** — where different things meet is where meaning is made
- **Multiple forms, one origin** — the central and defining idea

---

## The Logo

The Common Origin mark is two overlapping circles — an asymmetric Venn diagram.

**Anatomy:**
- The left circle is slightly smaller and partially cropped, making the composition directional: it reads left-to-right, from past to present, from origin to now
- Both circles are solid black fill — no outlines, no gradients, no decoration
- The wordmark "Common Origin" is set in white inside the right circle / overlap area — meaning lives at the intersection
- The wordmark typeface is a geometric, rounded, heavy sans-serif — warmer and more humanist than Inter. The weight and roundness of this type is a deliberate tonal signal: precise, but human.
- The mark works at any size from ~16px (two circles only, no text) to full lockup with wordmark

**Colour rule:**
- The logo uses pure black (#000000) and pure white (#FFFFFF)
- UI components in the design system must use near-black (#212529), not pure black — the logo is the only place #000000 appears
- This keeps the logo visually distinct from the UI system

**What the logo communicates:**
- Mathematical precision (pure geometry)
- Intersection and connection (overlapping forms)
- Directional movement (asymmetry, left-to-right)
- Human warmth within precision (rounded type, circular forms)

---

## Brand Identity Summary

| Dimension | Expression |
|---|---|
| **Tone** | Professional, considered, authentic — not corporate, not performing ambition |
| **Visual character** | Monochrome-first, minimal, editorial, technically precise |
| **Brand feel** | Quiet confidence — UI chrome is silent so content speaks |
| **Human quality** | Sharpness balanced by rounded forms and warmth |
| **Personality** | Precise, curious, creative, unhurried |
| **Anti-identity** | Not corporate, not hustle culture, not metric-driven, not decorative |

---

## What Common Origin Is Not

Agents must understand the negative space of this brand as much as the positive:

- **Not a SaaS product** — there is no growth target, no funnel to optimise
- **Not a corporate identity** — there is no committee, no brand guidelines by consensus
- **Not minimalism as aesthetic fashion** — minimal because everything earns its place, not because it looks cool
- **Not trying to look like any other studio** — reference systems (Stripe, Shopify) inform quality ceiling, not identity

---

## Products Using This Design System

| Product | Context |
|---|---|
| Common Origin personal site | Creative portfolio (music, art, design sections) — the richest visual reference; the live site IS this design system |
| A2UI | AI financial proof-of-concept — data-dense, financial context |
| Meal Agent | Agentic AI meal planning application |
| Smaller React projects | Various personal and creative projects |

**Important for agents:** The Common Origin personal site is built with this design system. The live site is not a separate reference — it is evidence of what the system produces. When making visual decisions, the live site is the ground truth.

---

## Tagline

> A creative studio.
